report_name: Biden Administration Releases New AI and Cybersecurity Guidelines for Critical Infrastructure

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
identities.description: A U.S. federal agency that published guidelines for critical infrastructure owners on securing AI systems in April 2024.

identities.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
identities.description: A U.S. federal agency that issued draft guidance on secure software development for generative AI and an AI Risk Management Framework profile in April 2024.

identities.name: National Security Agency
identities.description: A U.S. federal agency whose AI Security Center released guidance on hardening AI systems in April 2024.

identities.name: Biden Administration
identities.description: The executive branch of the U.S. government that issued a series of directives and frameworks on AI and cybersecurity in April 2024.

identities.name: U.S. Government
identities.description: The federal government of the United States, which released a series of directives and frameworks on AI and cybersecurity in April 2024 through its agencies.

identities.name: U.S. critical infrastructure owners and operators
identities.description: A target audience for the new AI and cybersecurity guidance released by U.S. government agencies.

identities.name: U.S. federal government agencies
identities.description: A target audience for the new AI and cybersecurity guidance, for whom compliance will be mandatory.

identities.name: Private sector companies
identities.description: A target audience for the new AI and cybersecurity guidance, particularly those developing, deploying, or using AI systems.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and AI research communities
identities.description: A target audience for the new AI and cybersecurity guidance.

relationships.source_name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
National Institute of Standards and Technology
